title: British Time Expressions You Need Every Day ⏰

vocab.definition: to start or continue doing something quickly and with energy
vocab.example: Let's crack on with the lesson.

vocab.definition: an unreasonably early or late hour of the day
vocab.example: I woke up at stupid o'clock this morning.
I haven't seen you in yonks!

vocab.definition: to spend time doing things that are not important without achieving much
vocab.example: Stop faffing around and get ready.
We go out for dinner now and then.

vocab.definition: approaching a certain age, time, or number
vocab.example: It is getting on for midnight.

lines.text: Hello everyone and welcome back to Sprout English.
Today we are looking at British time expressions that you will hear every day.
Let's crack on with the first phrase before we run out of time.
I had to wake up at stupid o'clock this morning to catch my train.
Honestly, I haven't been to this part of the city in absolute yonks.
If you stop faffing around, we might actually finish this work early.
We go down to the local pub now and then just to catch up.
Look at the clock, it is getting on for ten already, we should go.
